name: astrocam
plate_scale_notes: Nominal: Camera Module v2 stock lens, 62.2 deg horizontal / 3280 px. Not yet measured from a solve.
pole_prior_notes: Full-res Bayer pixel coords; see occlusion.json pole_prior_notes for provenance.
rotate_180_notes: Astrocam is mounted with the sensor inverted relative to the published frame; rotate before stretching to JPEG.
pedestal_notes: Sensor floor of the IMX219 raw output. Re-measured 2026-06-21 from deep-night (00-03 UTC) frame means across nights 2026-06-15..20 (6166 samples): global min mean 512.6, p0.1 512.7, p1 512.8, median 541. The earlier 2026-06-15 value of 519 was sampled too high (small/bright sample) and sat ABOVE the true dark-frame mean, so every deep-night frame fell below pedestal and the brightness chart floored at -1 stop. 512 sits just under the observed minimum. Astrocam stores raw 10-bit counts (no 16-bit bit-shift), so the pedestal is order-of-magnitude smaller than v3w's 4380.
night_layout_notes: YYYY-MM-DD/HH/MMSS.fits.fz (UTC dirs); night membership decided by DATE-OBS within the noon-rollover window.
sky_clear_max_stops_notes: Dark-trough brightness above which the night is called 'cloudy' (cloud-glow/light pollution) and the chart draws the clear/cloudy line. Per-sensor because 'stops above pedestal' is sensor-relative. astrocam (raw 10-bit, pedestal 512, only ~10.5 stops to twilight) sits at ~0-2 stops on a clear dark trough and ~6-7 on a fully cloudy night (e.g. 2026-06-15), so 4 stops divides them. NOT the 10 used for v3w, which has a 16-bit container and far more headroom.
state_notes: Night starts at sun -10 deg below horizon (ephem), matching eclipticam-v3w/v1 at the same site so all cameras transition together. Was the -12 default before 2026-06-20.
reference_nights_notes: Known dark/clear baseline overlaid on the combined-brightness chart (drawn coloured dashed). Same reference night as eclipticam-v3w. The latest night is drawn bold dashed black.
